title: Reversing Entries — Why and How
event: On December 31, Hollis Co. accrued $4,000 of salaries (payday is January 8, when $10,000 of total wages will be paid). Hollis uses reversing entries. Prepare the December 31 accrual, the January 1 reversing entry, and the January 8 payment entry.

lines.label: earned by 12/31
lines.why: December's work belongs in December's expense.

lines.label: owed at year-end
lines.trap: This payable is exactly what the reversing entry will erase on day one.

lines.label: liability erased
lines.why: The payable disappears before business even starts.
lines.trap: Reversing is OPTIONAL bookkeeping convenience — it changes no year's income, only the mechanics.

lines.label: temporary credit balance
lines.why: Salaries Expense now holds a 4,000 CREDIT balance — deliberately backwards.
lines.trap: The weird credit balance is the point: it pre-absorbs December's share of the coming payment.

entries.label: 3 — Jan 8: pay the full 10,000 with zero thinking

lines.label: full payment, no splitting
lines.why: The bookkeeper debits the whole check to expense — no need to remember any year-end split.
lines.trap: Without the reversal, this entry must split: Dr Payable 4,000 + Dr Expense 6,000 — and forgetting the split double-counts December.

lines.label: payday
lines.trap: Check the math: 10,000 debit − 4,000 credit balance = 6,000 net January expense. Exactly right, automatically.

memorize.body: Reverse only ACCRUALS (accrued expenses and revenues) — never deferrals like depreciation or prepaid adjustments.
The reversal creates a temporary 'wrong-way' balance on purpose. It self-corrects the moment the related cash entry posts.

questions.prompt: After the reversing entry, the Jan 8 payment of $10,000 is debited entirely to Salaries Expense. Net January expense = ?

distractors.feedback: You counted December's 4,000 in January again. The reversal pre-loaded a 4,000 credit, netting January to 6,000.
That's December's accrued piece. January's own expense is the remainder: 10,000 − 4,000 = 6,000.

build.scaffold: Accrue, reverse, pay. The reversal makes entry 3 mindless — that's its entire job.
